lines.gurmukhi: ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਕਰਿ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਮੇਰੇ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਉਰ ਧਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: kripaa kripaa kar tthaakur mere har har har ur dhaar |
lines.english: O my Lord and Master, please be merciful, merciful to me, that I may enshrine the Lord, Har, Har, Har, within my heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਜਨ ਕਰਣੀ ਊਤਮ ਹੈ ਹਰਿ ਕੀਰਤਿ ਜਗਿ ਬਿਸਥਾਰਿ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: har jan karanee aootam hai har keerat jag bisathaar |3|
lines.english: The lifestyle of the Lord's humble servant is exalted and sublime. He spreads the Kirtan of the Lord's Praises throughout the world. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਪੂਰਾ ਪਾਇਆ ਮਨਿ ਜਪਿਆ ਨਾਮੁ ਮੁਰਾਰਿ ॥੪॥੯॥
lines.translit: naanak satigur pooraa paaeaa man japiaa naam muraar |4|9|
lines.english: Nanak has found the Perfect True Guru; in his mind, he chants the Name of the Lord. ||4||9||
Malaar, Third Mehl, Second House:
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਗਿਰਹੀ ਕਿ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਉਦਾਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: eihu man girahee ki ihu man udaasee |
lines.english: Is this mind a householder, or is this mind a detached renunciate?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਅਵਰਨੁ ਸਦਾ ਅਵਿਨਾਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: ki ihu man avaran sadaa avinaasee |
lines.english: Is this mind beyond social class, eternal and unchanging?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਚੰਚਲੁ ਕਿ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਬੈਰਾਗੀ ॥
lines.translit: ki ihu man chanchal ki ihu man bairaagee |
lines.english: Is this mind fickle, or is this mind detached?

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਡਿਤ ਇਸੁ ਮਨ ਕਾ ਕਰਹੁ ਬੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: panddit is man kaa karahu beechaar |
lines.english: O Pandit, O religious scholar, reflect on this in your mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸੁ ਮਨ ਕਉ ਮਮਤਾ ਕਿਥਹੁ ਲਾਗੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: eis man kau mamataa kithahu laagee |1|
lines.english: How has this mind been gripped by possessiveness? ||1||
Enforcing His Order, He created the world.
The Creator has attached it to Maya and possessiveness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਵਰੁ ਕਿ ਬਹੁਤਾ ਪੜਹਿ ਉਠਾਵਹਿ ਭਾਰੁ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: avar ki bahutaa parreh utthaaveh bhaar |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Why do you read so many other things, and carry such a heavy load? ||1||Pause||
Remain forever in the Sanctuary of the Lord. ||2||
By Guru's Grace, understand this, O Siblings of Destiny.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਪੰਡਿਤੁ ਜੋ ਤਿਹਾਂ ਗੁਣਾ ਕੀ ਪੰਡ ਉਤਾਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: so panddit jo tihaan gunaa kee pandd utaarai |
lines.english: He alone is a Pandit, who sheds the load of the three qualities.
He accepts the Teachings of the True Guru.
Night and day, he chants the Name of the One Lord.
He offers his head to the True Guru.
He remains forever unattached in the state of Nirvaanaa.
He preaches that the One Lord is within all beings.
Such a Pandit is accepted in the Court of the Lord. ||3||
That person, whom the Lord forgives, is united with Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਤ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਕਵਨ ਬਿਧਿ ਕਰੇ ਕਿਆ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: kehat naanak kavan bidh kare kiaa koe |
lines.english: Says Nanak, what can anyone do?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਂ ਏਕੋ ਵੇਖੈ ਤਾਂ ਏਕੋ ਜਾਣੈ ॥
lines.translit: jaan eko vekhai taan eko jaanai |
lines.english: As he sees the One Lord, he knows the One Lord.
He finds eternal peace, here and hereafter. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋਈ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਜਾ ਕਉ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: soee mukat jaa kau kirapaa hoe |
lines.english: He alone is liberated, whom the Lord blesses with His Grace.
Night and day, he sings the Glorious Praises of the Lord.
The self-willed manmukhs wander lost in reincarnation, confused and deluded by doubt.
Malaar, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਸਤ੍ਰ ਬੇਦ ਕੀ ਫਿਰਿ ਕੂਕ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥੫॥੧॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: saasatr bed kee fir kook na hoe |5|1|10|
lines.english: Then, he no longer bothers with the proclamations of the Shaastras or the Vedas. ||5||1||10||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸੇਵਾ ਜਮ ਕੀ ਕਾਣਿ ਚੁਕਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: satigur sevaa jam kee kaan chukaaee |
lines.english: Serving the True Guru, the mortal's subservience to Death is ended.
The Messenger of Death constantly beats them and disgraces them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਮਿਲਿਆ ਮਹਲੁ ਘਰੁ ਪਾਈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: har prabh miliaa mehal ghar paaee |1|
lines.english: He meets the Lord God, and enters the Mansion of His Presence. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨਮੁ ਪਦਾਰਥੁ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਖੋਇਆ ਕਉਡੀ ਬਦਲੈ ਜਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: janam padaarath dubidhaa khoeaa kauddee badalai jaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: In duality, you are ruining and wasting this priceless human life. You trade it away in exchange for a shell. ||1||Pause||
O mortal, as Gurmukh, meditate on the Naam, the Name of the Lord.
The Gurmukh falls in love with the Lord, by His Grace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਭਗਤਿ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਉਰਿ ਧਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: antar bhagat har har ur dhaar |
lines.english: He enshrines loving devotion to the Lord, Har, Har, deep within his heart.
The Word of the Shabad carries him across the terrifying world-ocean.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਭਰਮਿ ਭੂਲੇ ਬਹੁ ਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: bin gur bharam bhoole bahu maaeaa |
lines.english: Without the Guru, so many wander lost and confused in Maya.
He appears true in the True Court of the Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਹੁ ਕਰਮ ਕਰੇ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਨਹੀ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: bahu karam kare satigur nahee paaeaa |
lines.english: Performing all sorts of rituals, they do not find the True Guru.
In the love of dualty, the self-willed manmukhs suffer in pain. ||3||
Egotism, possessiveness and attachment rise up and increase within them.
Chant the Word of the Guru's Shabad, and earn the true profit.
The Creator Himself is Inaccessible and Infinite.
The Lord is Independent, Ever-present, here and now.
